title: Celebrate Earth Day with this Artemis 2 shot | Space photo of the day for April 22, 2026
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 mission captured an image of Earth to celebrate Earth Day. The photo was featured as the Space Photo of the Day for April 22, 2026.

title: See the moon shining in the daytime sky this week: Here's where to look
summary: The moon will be visible in the daytime sky this week as it approaches the full phase on May 1. This phenomenon occurs as the moon's brightness increases before the full moon.

title: Spectacular photos of the 2026 Lyrid meteor shower captured from Earth and space
summary: Spectacular photos of the 2026 Lyrid meteor shower were captured from Earth and space, showing meteors blazing through Earth's atmosphere around the April 22 peak.

title: Mysterious rings around Uranus point to hidden moons orbiting the ice giant
summary: Scientists have discovered mysterious rings around Uranus that hint at hidden moons orbiting the ice giant. The findings deepen the intrigue surrounding the Uranian system.

title: The Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope, NASA's next great observatory, is finally complete
summary: NASA's Nancy Grace Roman Space Telescope is now complete and scheduled to launch in September. It aims to reveal previously unseen regions of the cosmos.

title: You've heard of the Space Force. Now there's a 'NASA Force' — but it's not about making space war
summary: NASA is launching a 'NASA Force' initiative to recruit industry talent for short-term roles, following the Artemis 2 moon mission. This program aims to bring in expertise from the private sector to support NASA's goals.

title: Bring nebulas and galaxies into focus with nearly $700 off this smart telescope
summary: The Unistellar Odyssey Pro smart telescope is being offered at a discount of nearly $700, making it more accessible for astrophotography enthusiasts to capture nebulas and galaxies despite light pollution.

title: The moon and Jupiter steal the show after sunset tonight
summary: A half moon will pass close to Jupiter on April 22, creating a striking celestial scene after sunset. This event highlights the alignment of the moon and Jupiter in the night sky.

title: NASA rolls out Artemis 3 SLS rocket's huge core stage to gear up for 2027 launch (photo)
summary: NASA has rolled out the core stage of the Artemis 3 SLS rocket from the Michoud Assembly Facility to the Kennedy Space Center in Florida, in preparation for a planned 2027 launch.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the program's timeline and potential impacts on future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: Seven space missions from four countries using five different launch vehicles are scheduled for the week of April 20, including GPS, Progress, and Starlink launches.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the failed launch of Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket. This marks a setback for AST SpaceMobile's satellite constellation plans.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused its booster for the first time, just five months after its second launch. This milestone marks a significant advancement in the company's partially reusable launch vehicle capabilities.

title: Vast unveils Astronaut Flight Suit and revolutionary Large Docking Adapter
summary: Vast introduced an astronaut flight suit and a large docking adapter, showcasing a blend of fashion and functionality. This marks progress in commercial space station development.

title: Ship 39 and Booster 19 Both Complete Full Engine Static Fires
summary: Ship 39 and Booster 19 have completed full engine static fire tests as part of the Artemis program's preparations. This follows the successful return of Orion, marking a key milestone for future missions.

title: Expedition 74 sees vehicle departures and a spacewalk during March
summary: Expedition 74 crew on the International Space Station conducted a spacewalk and saw vehicle departures in March. This marked another active month for the crew onboard the ISS.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9 to launch Starlink satellites, New Glenn to launch with flown booster
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 is set to launch Starlink satellites, while Blue Origin's New Glenn will debut with a previously flown booster. Six launches are planned globally this week.

title: Falcon 9 launches CRS NG-24 cargo ship to ISS
summary: SpaceX's Falcon 9 successfully launched the CRS NG-24 cargo mission to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows Northrop Grumman's recent CRS NG-23 mission completion.

title: Artemis II returns to Earth after historic 10-day mission around the Moon
summary: Artemis II completed a historic 10-day mission around the Moon, traveling over 800,000 km before returning to Earth. This marked a significant milestone in NASA's lunar exploration program.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news topic. No specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates 50 years with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements. The publication marks five decades of significant milestones in the European space sector.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to share feedback on space sector topics during the SME Forum 2026. The event includes an online preparatory forum in April-May and an in-person session at ESA Headquarters in Paris on June 8-9, 2026.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received at an optimal location. The achievement marks a significant milestone for the project.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. Findings reveal solar wind structures in the inner corona move 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses advancements in spacecraft pointing technology, likely focusing on improved precision or new methods for directional control. It may highlight a specific company or organization leading these innovations.
company: Not explicitly mentioned in the provided text

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels in microgravity during ESA's 86th parabolic flight campaign. The study, led by ULB and Khalifa University, showed that lasers could propel the aerogels with controllable thrust, unlocking potential for future satellite and solar sail applications.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: Graphene and lasers could revolutionize space propulsion by enabling solar sail steering and satellite position adjustments. An experiment simulating microgravity conditions demonstrated the potential of this technology.

title: A silicon chip that can do it all
summary: A breakthrough silicon chip has been developed that can perform multiple functions, potentially revolutionizing space industry applications. The chip's versatility could enable more efficient and cost-effective satellite and spacecraft systems.

title: Tracking Sats From the Sea
summary: The article discusses how satellites provide domain awareness to protect Navy ships and suggests that the Navy could assist in tracking satellites. It explores the mutual benefits of this collaboration.

title: ATMOS Space Cargo Raises €25.7M Series A
summary: ATMOS Space Cargo has secured €25.7 million in a Series A funding round to advance its commercial, institutional, and defense-capable space cargo services. The company plans to transition to regular operational service with its ATMOS WORKS and PHOENIX 3 initiatives.

title: Payload Field Guide: Golden Dome
summary: Golden Dome, initially announced in January 2025, is an ambitious US missile defense program that has attracted significant interest from companies aiming to compete for contracts. The program aims to enhance national security through advanced missile defense capabilities.

title: Opportunities Beyond the Moon Opened by CLPS
summary: NASA plans to launch monthly uncrewed missions to the Moon starting next year under the CLPS program, creating opportunities for various space industry companies beyond traditional lunar-focused firms.

title: Economics of Orbital Data Centers Report: Part 1
summary: The article initiates a four-part report analyzing the economics of orbital data centers, highlighting the push for compute resources in space due to terrestrial bottlenecks.

title: NASA’s 2028 Moon Landing Has A Spacesuit Problem, New Report Says
summary: NASA's planned 2028 Moon landing faces delays due to issues with spacesuit development, according to a new report from the Office of the Inspector General. The setback threatens the agency's timeline for returning astronauts to the lunar surface.

title: Latvia To Join Artemis Accords Today
summary: Latvia is set to join the Artemis Accords today, marking another country aligning with international space exploration norms. The move aims to transition from symbolic support to active contributions to NASA's Artemis program.

title: SpaceX Won A Mars Mission That Might Get Cancelled
summary: SpaceX has been awarded a contract to launch the ESA's Rosalind Franklin rover to Mars by late 2028 for $175.7M. However, NASA's potential cancellation of its participation in the mission due to the White House's FY2027 budget request casts uncertainty on the project's future.

title: Rocket Lab Enters the Thruster Market with Gauss
summary: Rocket Lab is entering the thruster market with its new Gauss product line. The company aims to address scalability concerns by vertically integrating thruster production.

title: Axiom Space’s Suit Set to Fly in 2027
summary: Axiom Space is developing its first qualification spacesuit, with plans to fly it in 2027. The company's chief astronaut confirmed the suit's production is ongoing despite external factors.

title: Pentagon to Move Forward On AEP Upgrades for GPS, As DoD Cancels GPS OCX
summary: The Pentagon has decided to cancel the GPS Next Generation Operational Control System (OCX) developed by RTX and instead proceed with upgrades to the existing ground system for GPS, which is built by Lockheed Martin.

title: Intellian Details New Terminals for Aviation and WGS Network
summary: Intellian Technologies introduced two new terminals, including its first aviation terminal and a tactical flyaway terminal. The company is expanding its satellite technology offerings with these new products.

title: Eutelsat Retires GEO Satellite 139 West A to Graveyard Orbit
summary: Eutelsat has retired its GEO communications satellite Eutelsat 139 West A after 22 years of service, moving it to a graveyard orbit. The satellite's retirement was announced in a statement released on Tuesday.

title: Air India Chief Digital Officer Talks IFC Roadmap at AIX: Video Interview
summary: Air India's Chief Digital and Technology Officer discussed the airline's In-Flight Connectivity (IFC) roadmap at the Aircraft Interiors Expo (AIX) in Hamburg. The interview was conducted by Via Satellite's Senior Editorial Director Mark Holmes.

title: Airline, OEM, Satellite Players Say IFC Experience is in the ‘Details’
summary: Airlines, OEMs, and satellite providers are focusing on perfecting in-flight connectivity (IFC) experiences to meet rising customer expectations, as discussed at Aircraft Interiors (AIX). The challenges lie in the intricate details of the technology and service design.

title: Poland Invests in New Defense GEO Satellite Built by Thales, Airbus, and Radmor
summary: Poland has signed a contract with Thales, Airbus, and Radmor to develop a new Geostationary Orbit (GEO) defense satellite. This investment strengthens Poland's space defense capabilities.

title: New Glenn Grounded and AST SpaceMobile Satellite Lost After Launch Anomaly
summary: BlueBird 7 satellite was lost due to a New Glenn launch anomaly, causing AST SpaceMobile to file an insurance claim. The New Glenn rocket has been grounded pending investigation.

title: Ovzon Launches New Mobile Terminal in Small Form Factor
summary: Swedish satellite operator Ovzon has launched a new mobile satellite terminal, the Ovzon T8, designed for mobility across air, land, and sea. The terminal features a small form factor for enhanced portability.

title: Inside SatEnlight’s Spiral Staircase of Lasers: Interview with Startup Space Winner Matteo Vismara
summary: SatEnlight, an Italian optical communications company based in Milan, uses a unique multi-layered laser beam arrangement for its technology. The company was featured in an interview with its founder Matteo Vismara as a Startup Space winner.

title: IFC News Roundup from AIX
summary: The AIX Aircraft Interiors Expo highlighted announcements from satellite companies promoting in-flight connectivity (IFC) solutions to airlines. Via Satellite summarized key developments in this sector during the event.
